pages.title: Wooden stave
pages.text: The basic stave is a short diagonal recipe: plank in the upper-right, stick in the lower-left.$(br)It is not the long handle shape used by vanilla tools.

pages.title: 2x2 layout
pages.text: A crafting table or your inventory grid both work:$(br2)$(l)Empty  Plank$()$(br)$(l)Stick   Empty$()$(br2)If you place a vertical stick and plank, that is not this mod's stave recipe.
Think short wand, not vanilla tool handle.

pages.title: Upgrade shape
pages.text: Upgrades use the same diagonal shape: next-tier material in the upper-right, tribulation-cleared stave in the lower-left.$(br2)Only a stave with full brush momentum and a cleared $(l)劫$() can upgrade.

pages.title: Brush momentum
pages.text: Every successful implemented glyph adds 1 brush momentum to the stave you are holding. Unimplemented glyphs say “Glyph unopened” and do not cast, gain momentum, or cost durability.$(br2)Clearing an $(l)入$() Ink Arena adds a larger chunk. When the stave says its realm is full, you can write $(l)劫$().

pages.title: After tribulation
pages.text: After writing $(l)劫$() and clearing the trial, the current stave gains an upgrade-ready mark.$(br2)Then craft with the short-stave shape: stone, copper ingot, iron ingot, gold ingot, diamond, and netherite ingot each move to the next tier. The new stave starts with zero momentum.

pages.title: Anvil repair
pages.text: Put the stave you want to keep in the first anvil slot, and another same-tier stave in the second slot. The output repairs the first stave and keeps its momentum and tribulation state.$(br2)The second stave is only material. Its name, momentum, and other data do not transfer.

pages.title: Tier feel
pages.text: Wood is entry-level. Stone becomes steadier. Copper is the middle step. Iron is the workhorse. Gold leans toward burst. Diamond unlocks the full high-pressure playstyle. Netherite is the current top tier.

pages.title: If crafting fails
pages.text: If the recipe has no result, one of three things is usually wrong: the stave momentum is not full, $(l)劫$() has not been cleared, or the material/stave positions are swapped.$(br2)If durability is the only problem, repair it on an anvil with a same-tier stave. You do not need to repeat tribulation.

pages.title: Which stave?
pages.text: Daily survival: wood to copper is enough.$(br2)Caving and normal combat: iron is the most comfortable.$(br2)Ink Arenas and high-pressure fights: diamond starts to feel right.$(br2)To write a glyph at its fullest: use netherite.
